promptText: Based on the Rival's behaviour and your intelligence, what kind of adversary do you believe you are facing?
unsureText: Insufficient evidence — I am not yet ready to commit to a type.

typeDescriptions.OPPORTUNIST: A greedy opportunist: probes for cheap gains and only fights when it looks easy. Deterred by demonstrated denial and resolve.
typeDescriptions.PRESSURED_EXPANSIONIST: A pressured expansionist acting under a closing window: internal pressure drives escalation, so early deterrence matters most.
typeDescriptions.SECURITY_SEEKER: A security-seeker arming out of fear: responds to a spiral of threat. Reassurance and defensive posture cool it; offensive build-ups inflame it.

tellSheets.OPPORTUNIST: Tell: probes track your perceived resolve, and it backs down when enforced. It never escalated purely because you built defenses — only when it smelled weakness.
tellSheets.PRESSURED_EXPANSIONIST: Tell: probe severity rose with the calendar, not with your posture. Its war-utility climbed even in quarters you were firm — the window, not you, set the tempo.
tellSheets.SECURITY_SEEKER: Tell: its arming tracked your threat signature. Quarters where you reassured or built purely defensive denial cooled it; offensive-coded punishment and mobilizations spiked its threat perception.
